questions.assertion: When a partner retires or dies, the partnership firm can be dissolved.
questions.reason: Dissolution of a partnership firm is the process of bringing the partnership to an end.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because when a partner retires or dies, it can lead to the dissolution of the partnership firm due to the process of bringing it to an end.

questions.assertion: In a dissolution of a partnership firm, the assets are distributed among the partners in the ratio of their capitals.
questions.reason: The assets are distributed in the ratio of their capitals because it is based on the principle of 'share the profits and losses in the same ratio'.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the assets are distributed in the ratio of their capitals as per the principle of 'share the profits and losses in the same ratio'.

questions.assertion: Dissolution of a partnership firm can be voluntary or involuntary.
questions.reason: A partnership firm can be dissolved voluntarily when all partners agree to end the partnership, and it can be dissolved involuntarily due to the death or retirement of a partner.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because a partnership firm can be dissolved voluntarily or involuntarily as per the given reason.

questions.assertion: When a partnership firm is dissolved, the liabilities are paid first.
questions.reason: The liabilities are paid first because it is the legal responsibility of the partners to pay the debts of the firm.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the liabilities are paid first as per the legal responsibility of the partners to pay the debts of the firm.

questions.assertion: The assets of a partnership firm are distributed among the partners in the ratio of their capitals when the firm is dissolved.
questions.reason: This is not true because the assets are distributed in the ratio of their capitals plus or minus their drawings.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the assets are distributed in the ratio of their capitals plus or minus their drawings when the firm is dissolved.

questions.assertion: When a partnership firm is dissolved, the profits and losses are shared in the same ratio as the capital.
questions.reason: This is true because the profits and losses are shared in the same ratio as the capital, which is based on the principle of 'share the profits and losses in the same ratio'.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the profits and losses are shared in the same ratio as the capital as per the principle of 'share the profits and losses in the same ratio'.

questions.assertion: A partnership firm can be dissolved if one of the partners is declared insolvent.
questions.reason: This is true because a partnership firm can be dissolved if one of the partners is declared insolvent, as it affects the partnership.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because a partnership firm can be dissolved if one of the partners is declared insolvent.

questions.assertion: When a partnership firm is dissolved, the capital of the partners is returned to them.
questions.reason: This is not true because the capital of the partners is not returned to them when the firm is dissolved.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the capital of the partners is not returned to them when the firm is dissolved.

questions.assertion: The dissolution of a partnership firm involves the sale of the assets and the payment of liabilities.
questions.reason: This is true because the dissolution of a partnership firm involves the sale of the assets and the payment of liabilities, which is a part of the process of winding up the firm.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This option is correct because the dissolution of a partnership firm involves the sale of the assets and the payment of liabilities.

questions.assertion: The dissolution of a partnership firm is the same as the winding up of a partnership firm.
